heading: Property used in the trade or business and involuntary conversions
label: 1231 Property used in the trade or business and involuntary conversions
notes: Amendments2017—Subsec. (b)(1)(C). inserted “a patent, invention, model or design (whether or not patented), a secret formula or process,” before “a copyright”.Pub. L. 115–972014—Subsec. (c)(2)(A). struck out “beginning after ” after “years”.Pub. L. 113–295December 31, 19811999—Subsec. (b)(1)(C), (D). substituted “section 1221(a)” for “section 1221”.Pub. L. 106–1701984—Subsec. (a). , (e), substituted “6 months” for “1 year” wherever appearing, applicable to property acquired after , and before . See Effective Date of 1984 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 98–369, § 1001(b)(15)June 22, 1984Jan. 1, 1988, amended subsec. (a) generally, substituting pars. (1) to (4), for “If, during the taxable year, the recognized gains on sales or exchanges of property used in the trade or business, plus the recognized gains from the compulsory or involuntary conversion (as a result of destruction in whole or in part, theft or seizure, or an exercise of the power of requisition or condemnation or the threat or imminence thereof) of property used in the trade or business and capital assets held for more than 1 year into other property or money, exceed the recognized losses from such sales, exchanges, and conversions, such gains and losses shall be considered as gains and losses from sales or exchanges of capital assets held for more than 1 year. If such gains do not exceed such losses, such gains and losses shall not be considered as gains and losses from sales or exchanges of capital assets. For purposes of this subsection—Pub. L. 98–369, § 711(c)(2)(A)(iii)“(1) in determining under this subsection whether gains exceed losses, the gains described therein shall be included only if and to the extent taken into account in computing gross income and the losses described therein shall be included only if and to the extent taken into account in computing taxable income, except that section 1211 shall not apply; and“(2) losses (including losses not compensated for by insurance or otherwise) upon the destruction, in whole or in part, theft or seizure, or requisition or condemnation of (A) property used in the trade or business or (B) capital assets held for more than 1 year shall be considered losses from a compulsory or involuntary conversion.In the case of any involuntary conversion (subject to the provisions of this subsection but for this sentence) arising from fire, storm, shipwreck, or other casualty, or from theft, of any property used in the trade or business or of any capital asset held for more than 1 year, this subsection shall not apply to such conversion (whether resulting in gain or loss) if during the taxable year the recognized losses from such conversions exceed the recognized gains from such conversions.”Subsec. (b)(1), (4). , (e), substituted “6 months” for “1 year”, applicable to property acquired after , and before . See Effective Date of 1984 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 98–369, § 1001(b)(15)June 22, 1984Jan. 1, 1988Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 98–369, § 176(a)1981—Subsec. (b)(1)(D). substituted “paragraph (5)” for “paragraph (6)”.Pub. L. 97–341978—Subsec. (b)(1)(D). added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 95–6001976—Subsecs. (a), (b)(1), (4). , provided that “9 months” would be changed to “1 year” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1402(b)(2), provided that in subsecs. (a), first and last sentences, (a)(2), and (b)(1), (4), “6 months” would be changed to “9 months” for taxable years beginning in 1977.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1402(b)(1)(R)1969—Subsec. (a). , provided that casualty (or theft) losses with respect to depreciable property and real estate used in trade or business and capital assets held for the production of income as well as personal assets are to be consolidated with casualty (or theft) gains with respect to this type of property and if the casualty losses exceed the casualty gains, the net loss is treated as an ordinary loss without regard to whether there may be noncasualty gains under this section, but, if the casualty gains exceed the casualty losses, the net gain is treated as a gain under this section and must be consolidated with other gains and losses under this section.Pub. L. 91–172, § 516(b)Subsec. (b)(1)(C). , inserted reference to a letter or memorandum.Pub. L. 91–172, § 514(b)(2)Subsec. (b)(3). , redesignated existing provisions as subpar. (B) and added subpar. (A).Pub. L. 91–172, § 212(b)(1)1964—Subsec. (b)(2). inserted reference to iron ore in text, and to domestic iron ore in heading.Pub. L. 88–2721958—Subsec. (a). inserted provision respecting casualty losses sustained upon certain uninsured property.Pub. L. 85–866

children.label: A
children.text: the section 1231 gains for any taxable year, exceed

children.label: B
children.text: the section 1231 losses for such taxable year,

children.label: A
children.text: the section 1231 gains for any taxable year, do not exceed

children.label: B
children.text: the section 1231 losses for such taxable year,

children.label: i
children.text: any recognized gain on the sale or exchange of property used in the trade or business, and

children.label: I
children.text: property used in the trade or business, or

children.label: II
children.text: any capital asset which is held for more than 1 year and is held in connection with a trade or business or a transaction entered into for profit.

children.heading: Section 1231 loss
children.label: B Section 1231 loss
children.text: The term “section 1231 loss” means any recognized loss from a sale or exchange or conversion described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: the section 1231 gains shall be included only if and to the extent taken into account in computing gross income, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the section 1231 losses shall be included only if and to the extent taken into account in computing taxable income, except that section 1211 shall not apply.

children.label: i
children.text: property used in the trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: capital assets which are held for more than 1 year and are held in connection with a trade or business or a transaction entered into for profit,

children.label: i
children.text: property used in the trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any capital asset which is held for more than 1 year and is held in connection with a trade or business or a transaction entered into for profit,

children.heading: Definition of property used in the trade or business
children.label: b Definition of property used in the trade or business
A
children.text: property of a kind which would properly be includible in the inventory of the taxpayer if on hand at the close of the taxable year,

children.label: B
children.text: property held by the taxpayer primarily for sale to customers in the ordinary course of his trade or business,

children.label: C
children.text: a patent, invention, model or design (whether or not patented), a secret formula or process, a copyright, a literary, musical, or artistic composition, a letter or memorandum, or similar property, held by a taxpayer described in paragraph (3) of section 1221(a), or

children.label: D
children.text: a publication of the United States Government (including the Congressional Record) which is received from the United States Government, or any agency thereof, other than by purchase at the price at which it is offered for sale to the public, and which is held by a taxpayer described in paragraph (5) of section 1221(a).

children.heading: Timber, coal, or domestic iron ore
children.label: 2 Timber, coal, or domestic iron ore
children.text: Such term includes timber, coal, and iron ore with respect to which section 631 applies.

children.label: A
children.text: cattle and horses, regardless of age, held by the taxpayer for draft, breeding, dairy, or sporting purposes, and held by him for 24 months or more from the date of acquisition, and

children.label: B
children.text: other livestock, regardless of age, held by the taxpayer for draft, breeding, dairy, or sporting purposes, and held by him for 12 months or more from the date of acquisition.

children.heading: Unharvested crop
children.label: 4 Unharvested crop
children.text: In the case of an unharvested crop on land used in the trade or business and held for more than 1 year, if the crop and the land are sold or exchanged (or compulsorily or involuntarily converted) at the same time and to the same person, the crop shall be considered as “property used in the trade or business.”

children.heading: Recapture of net ordinary losses
children.label: c Recapture of net ordinary losses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The net section 1231 gain for any taxable year shall be treated as ordinary income to the extent such gain does not exceed the non-recaptured net section 1231 losses.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate amount of the net section 1231 losses for the 5 most recent preceding taxable years, over

children.label: B
children.text: the portion of such losses taken into account under paragraph (1) for such preceding taxable years.

children.label: A
children.text: the section 1231 gains, over

children.label: B
children.text: the section 1231 losses.

children.label: A
children.text: the section 1231 losses, over

children.label: B
children.text: the section 1231 gains.

children.heading: Special rules
children.label: 5 Special rules
children.text: For purposes of determining the amount of the net section 1231 gain or loss for any taxable year, the rules of paragraph (4) of subsection (a) shall apply.
